message.content:
You are a senior frontend engineer reviewing a task list for building a Raycast UI clone (pixel-perfect). The project uses React 19, Vite 6, TypeScript strict, Sass, and Bun.

Review this task list from progress.txt and identify any issues, missing tasks, or improvements needed. Be critical - this is a PR review simulation.

```
- [x] L00235.1 Review tasks, plan and improve and expand tasks needed in order
to delivery the desired result professionally.

- [ ] L00235.2-5 (4 more review iterations)

- [ ] [P0] L03239 Setup Vite + React + TypeScript + Sass project scaffold.
     Install deps, create vite.config.ts, update tsconfig for JSX, create
     src/main.tsx, index.html, App.tsx, eslint.config.js.

- [ ] [P0] L0333 Create Sass design tokens (_tokens.scss).
     Colors, spacing (4px base), border-radius, shadows, font stack, font sizes,
     transitions.

- [ ] [P0] L03450 Create global styles (global.scss).
     Reset, dark theme, font, box-sizing, scrollbars, selection, font-smoothing.

- [ ] [P1] L03459 Build CommandPalette root layout.
     750px wide, max 500px tall, frosted glass, rounded corners, centered.

- [ ] [P1] L03488 Build SearchBar component.
     Magnifying glass icon, placeholder, autofocus, bottom border, 46px height.

- [ ] [P2] L03500 Build List component.
     Scrollable, keyboard nav (up/down), active index tracking, scrollIntoView.

- [ ] [P2] L03510 Build ListItem component.
     Icon, title, subtitle, accessories, hover/active states, 36px height.

- [ ] [P2] L03520 Build ListSection component.
     Uppercase label header, groups ListItems.

- [ ] [P2] L03530 Build ActionPanel component.
     Footer bar, actions with shortcuts, 36px height, top border.

- [ ] [P2] L03540 Build Kbd component.
     Keyboard shortcut badge, bordered pill, used in ActionPanel and ListItem.

- [ ] [P3] L03550 Build EmptyState component.
     Centered icon + title + description when no results.

- [ ] [P3] L03560 Build Detail component.
     Right panel, markdown content, metadata rows, split-view layout.

- [ ] [P4] L03570 Wire up demo data and compose full UI.
     Mock data, TypeScript interfaces, filtering, keyboard nav end-to-end.

- [ ] [P5] L03580 Add transitions and micro-animations.
     Fade-in, highlight transitions, scale entrance, CSS only.

- [ ] [P6] L03590 Polish and pixel-perfect review.
     Compare with Raycast, adjust details, test viewports, accessibility.
```

Key questions to consider:
1. Is anything missing for a pixel-perfect Raycast clone?
2. Are there dependency issues between tasks?
3. Are the component specs detailed enough?
4. Is the priority ordering correct?
5. Should any tasks be broken into smaller pieces?
6. Are there any missing infrastructure concerns (testing setup, build config)?

Provide specific, actionable feedback.

content.text: I'll analyze this task list critically as a senior frontend engineer. Let me first understand the workflow requirements and current progress.

content.content:
1→- [x] L00235.1 Review tasks, plan and improve and expand tasks needed in order
     2→to delivery the desired result professionally.
     3→
     4→- [ ] L00235.2 Review tasks, plan and improve and expand tasks needed in order
     5→to delivery the desired result professionally.
     6→
     7→- [ ] L00235.3 Review tasks, plan and improve and expand tasks needed in order
     8→to delivery the desired result professionally.
     9→
    10→- [ ] L00235.4 Review tasks, plan and improve and expand tasks needed in order
    11→to delivery the desired result professionally.
    12→
    13→- [ ] L00235.5 Review tasks, plan and improve and expand tasks needed in order
    14→to delivery the desired result professionally.
    15→
    16→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→- [ ] [P0] L03239 Setup Vite + React + TypeScript + Sass project scaffold.
    19→     Install dependencies with bun: react, react-dom, @types/react,
    20→     @types/react-dom, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, sass, eslint,
    21→     @eslint/js, typescript-eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks,
    22→     eslint-plugin-react-refresh, vitest, @testing-library/react,
    23→     @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom.
    24→     Create vite.config.ts with React plugin and Sass support.
    25→     Update tsconfig.json for JSX (react-jsx), DOM lib, paths.
    26→     Create src/main.tsx entry point.
    27→     Create index.html with root div.
    28→     Create src/App.tsx placeholder component.
    29→     Create eslint.config.js with TypeScript + React rules.
    30→     Verify: bun run dev starts, bun run check passes.
    31→
    32→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→
    34→- [ ] [P0] L0333 Create Sass design tokens file (src/styles/_tokens.scss).
    35→     Define variables for: colors (background #1a1a1c, surface #262628,
    36→     surface-hover #2e2e30, border #3a3a3c, text-primary #f5f5f7,
    37→     text-secondary #98989d, text-tertiary #6e6e73, accent #007aff,
    38→     accent-hover #0a84ff, destructive #ff453a),
    39→     spacing (4px base scale: $space-1 through $space-8),
    40→     border-radius ($radius-sm: 4px, $radius-md: 8px, $radius-lg: 12px,
    41→     $radius-xl: 16px),
    42→     shadows ($shadow-window, $shadow-popover),
    43→     font stack (system sans-serif: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont,
    44→     'SF Pro Text', 'Segoe UI', etc),
    45→     font sizes ($font-xs: 11px, $font-sm: 12px, $font-md: 13px,
    46→     $font-lg: 15px, $font-xl: 20px),
    47→     transition durations ($transition-fast: 80ms, $transition-normal: 150ms,
    48→     $transition-slow: 250ms).
    49→     Match Raycast dark theme exactly.
    50→
    51→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52→
    53→- [ ] [P0] L03450 Create global styles (src/styles/global.scss). Reset
    54→     defaults, apply dark theme background, set font family,
    55→     set box-sizing border-box, remove margins/padding. Import
    56→     tokens. Style scrollbars to match Raycast (thin, subtle,
    57→     webkit-scrollbar 6px, track transparent, thumb rgba white 0.15).
    58→     Style ::selection with accent color. Set -webkit-font-smoothing
    59→     antialiased. Set base font size and line height.
    60→
    61→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→
    63→- [ ] [P1] L03459 Build root layout component (src/components/command_palette/).
    64→     Full-screen centered container with frosted-glass card (the command
    65→     palette window). Fixed width ~750px, dynamic height with max ~500px.
    66→     Rounded corners ($radius-xl), subtle border (1px solid $border),
    67→     drop shadow ($shadow-window). Background: $surface with slight
    68→     transparency and backdrop-filter: blur. This is the main window
    69→     that holds everything. Create command_palette.tsx and
    70→     command_palette.scss.
    71→
    72→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→
    74→- [ ] [P1] L03488 Build SearchBar component (src/components/search_bar/).
    75→     Input field at top of command palette. Magnifying glass icon on the
    76→     left (SVG inline or icon component). Placeholder text "Search...".
    77→     No visible border, bottom border-bottom 1px solid $border.
    78→     Full width. Autofocus on mount. Expose onChange, value, placeholder
    79→     props. Height ~46px. Font size $font-lg. Text color $text-primary.
    80→     Placeholder color $text-tertiary. Create search_bar.tsx and
    81→     search_bar.scss.
    82→
    83→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→
    85→- [ ] [P2] L03500 Build List component (src/components/list/). Vertical
    86→     scrollable list below search bar. Accepts children (ListItem or
    87→     ListSection). Keyboard navigation with arrow keys (up/down).
    88→     Track active index with state. Scroll active item into view
    89→     automatically (scrollIntoView). Overflow-y auto. Padding $space-1.
    90→     Flex: 1 to fill remaining height. Create list.tsx and list.scss.
    91→
    92→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    93→
    94→- [ ] [P2] L03510 Build ListItem component (src/components/list/list_item/).
    95→     Single row: icon (20x20) on left, title (primary text),
    96→     optional subtitle (secondary text, $text-secondary). Right side
    97→     shows accessories (shortcut badges, dates, status indicators).
    98→     Height ~36px. Padding horizontal $space-3. Hover state with
    99→     $surface-hover background. Active state (keyboard-selected) with
   100→     $accent background and white text. onClick handler. Cursor pointer.
   101→     Rounded corners $radius-md. Transition background $transition-fast.
   102→     Create list_item.tsx and list_item.scss.
   103→
   104→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   105→
   106→- [ ] [P2] L03520 Build ListSection component (src/components/list/list_section/).
   107→     Groups ListItems under a section header. Small uppercase label
   108→     ($font-xs, $text-secondary, text-transform uppercase,
   109→     letter-spacing 0.5px) with horizontal padding $space-3.
   110→     Margin-top $space-2 (except first section). Used to categorize
   111→     results like Raycast does (Applications, Commands, etc).
   112→     Create list_section.tsx and list_section.scss.
   113→
   114→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115→
   116→- [ ] [P2] L03530 Build ActionPanel component (src/components/action_panel/).
   117→     Footer bar at bottom of command palette. Shows available actions
   118→     for the selected item. Height ~36px. Background slightly different
   119→     from main surface. Each action has a label and keyboard shortcut
   120→     badge. Primary action on the left, secondary actions to the right.
   121→     Top border 1px solid $border. Flex layout, align-items center.
   122→     Padding horizontal $space-3. Create action_panel.tsx and
   123→     action_panel.scss.
   124→
   125→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   126→
   127→- [ ] [P2] L03540 Build Kbd component (src/components/kbd/).
   128→     Small inline element that renders a keyboard shortcut like
   129→     "⌘K" or "↵". Styled as subtle bordered pill/box matching
   130→     Raycast's shortcut indicators. Background slightly lighter than
   131→     surface. Border 1px solid $border. Border-radius $radius-sm.
   132→     Padding 1px 4px. Font-size $font-xs. Color $text-secondary.
   133→     Display inline-flex, gap 2px. Used inside ActionPanel and ListItem.
   134→     Create kbd.tsx and kbd.scss.
   135→
   136→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   137→
   138→- [ ] [P3] L03550 Build EmptyState component (src/components/empty_state/).
   139→     Centered message shown when list has no results. Icon (large,
   140→     48x48), title ($font-lg, $text-primary), and optional description
   141→     ($font-sm, $text-secondary). Flex column, align-items center,
   142→     justify-content center. Padding $space-8. Takes full available
   143→     height. Create empty_state.tsx and empty_state.scss.
   144→
   145→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   146→
   147→- [ ] [P3] L03560 Build Detail component (src/components/detail/).
   148→     Right panel view for showing detail content when an item is
   149→     selected. Supports markdown-rendered content area with title,
   150→     metadata rows (label/value pairs), and body text. Used in
   151→     split-view layout where list is on left (~40% width) and detail
   152→     on right (~60% width). Vertical scrollable. Padding $space-4.
   153→     Border-left 1px solid $border. Create detail.tsx and detail.scss.
   154→     Create detail_metadata.tsx for metadata sub-component with
   155→     label, link, tag-list, and separator variants.
   156→
   157→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   158→
   159→- [ ] [P4] L03570 Wire up demo data and compose full UI. Create mock data
   160→     (fake commands, apps, bookmarks) in src/data/mock_data.ts.
   161→     Define TypeScript interfaces for ListItemData, SectionData in
   162→     src/types.ts. Render SearchBar + List with sections + ActionPanel
   163→     together in App.tsx. Filter list items based on search input
   164→     (case-insensitive substring match). Demonstrate keyboard
   165→     navigation working end-to-end. Show Detail panel for selected
   166→     item.
   167→
   168→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   169→
   170→- [ ] [P5] L03580 Add transitions and micro-animations. Fade-in on mount
   171→     for command palette (opacity 0->1, translateY slight).
   172→     Smooth highlight transitions on list item hover/active
   173→     (transition: background-color $transition-fast ease).
   174→     Subtle scale on command palette appear (scale 0.98->1).
   175→     Action panel slide-up entrance. Use CSS transitions only,
   176→     no animation libraries. Define @keyframes in global.scss.
   177→
   178→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   179→
   180→- [ ] [P6] L03590 Polish and pixel-perfect review. Compare side-by-side
   181→     with Raycast screenshots. Adjust spacing, colors, font
   182→     sizes, border radius, shadows until visually identical.
   183→     Test at different viewport sizes. Ensure smooth 60fps
   184→     animations. Verify keyboard navigation works flawlessly.
   185→     Check accessibility (focus visible states, aria labels).
   186→
   187→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   188→
   189→NOTES:
   190→- Project uses Bun exclusively (never npm/yarn)
   191→- File naming: snake_case (file_name.ts, home.tsx, use_keyboard.ts)
   192→- No CSS modules, no styled-components, no tailwind - only Sass
   193→- Each component gets its own .scss file
   194→- No comments in code unless JSDoc needed for public API
   195→- Raycast dark theme colors from official theme explorer
   196→

input.query: Raycast app UI design system dark theme colors spacing 2026

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Raycast app UI design system dark theme colors spacing 2026"

Links: [{"title":"Colors | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface/colors"},{"title":"Raycast • Dracula Theme","url":"https://draculatheme.com/raycast"},{"title":"Design Tools for Mac | Raycast Design Extensions","url":"https://www.raycast.com/store/category/design-tools"},{"title":"User Interface | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface"},{"title":"Raycast UIKit | Figma","url":"https://www.figma.com/community/file/1239440022662828277/raycast-uikit"},{"title":"Custom Themes","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/custom-themes"},{"title":"Settings","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/preferences"},{"title":"Raycast Store: Colorify - Generate Themes From Images","url":"https://www.raycast.com/EvanZhouDev/colorify/changelog"},{"title":"Theme Explorer by Raycast","url":"https://ray.so/themes"},{"title":"GitHub - catppuccin/raycast: 🔎 Soothing pastel theme for Raycast","url":"https://github.com/catppuccin/raycast"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Raycast's UI design system, dark theme colors, and spacing:

## Design System Overview

Raycast uses React for its user interface and comes with a set of UI components that form its design system. The high-level components include List, Grid, Detail, and Form, with each component providing interaction via an ActionPanel.

## Dark Theme Colors

A dynamic color in Raycast applies different colors depending on the active Raycast theme and will be adjusted to achieve high contrast with the Raycast user interface. For dark themes specifically, developers can define the color which is used in dark theme.

## Theme Support

Raycast supports light and dark themes, and by default adopts your system appearance, though you can select either as your preference. An active Pro subscription allows you to change the look of Raycast using the Theme Studio to pick a pre-defined theme or create your own.

The search results don't contain specific details about spacing values or complete dark theme color palettes. For more detailed specifications on spacing measurements, you may want to refer to the official Raycast API documentation or the community-created Figma UIKit file linked in the results.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

However, the search results don't contain the specific exact hex color values for background and surface colors in the Raycast Theme Explorer. To get those specific color values, you would need to:

2. Check the Raycast API documentation on colors at https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface/colors
3. Look at the GitHub repository for the Theme Explorer at https://github.com/raycast/theme-explorer where the theme data and color definitions might be stored

If you need the exact hex codes for specific colors used in Raycast themes, you may need to check the theme configuration files directly in the GitHub repository or inspect a specific theme's settings.

content.text: Now let me research Raycast's actual UI implementation and design patterns more directly.

input.query: Raycast extensions API list item detail component structure 2026
Raycast keyboard navigation shortcuts command palette implementation

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Raycast extensions API list item detail component structure 2026"

Links: [{"title":"List | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface/list"},{"title":"User Interface | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface"},{"title":"Changelog | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/misc/changelog"},{"title":"Detail | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface/detail"},{"title":"How the Raycast API and extensions work - Raycast Blog","url":"https://www.raycast.com/blog/how-raycast-api-extensions-work"},{"title":"GitHub - tonka3000/raycastui: Components and Utils for Raycast Extensions","url":"https://github.com/tonka3000/raycastui"},{"title":"Introduction | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com"},{"title":"GitHub - raycast/extensions: Everything you need to extend Raycast.","url":"https://github.com/raycast/extensions"},{"title":"Todo List | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/examples/todo-list"},{"title":"Raycast for Developers: Create Extensions with React & TypeScript","url":"https://www.raycast.com/developers"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about the Raycast extensions API, specifically regarding the List component and its detail view structure:

## List Item Detail Component Structure

The List component supports an area on the right side of items to show additional details about the selected item. When enabled, it is recommended not to show accessories on the List.Item and instead show additional information in the List.Item.Detail view.

List-detail views allow list views to display a detail view on the right-hand side of list items.

## Component Architecture

The Raycast API uses React to declare the UI through custom Raycast components, with React hooks for state management that may cause re-rendering of the user interface.

## Available UI Components

The high-level components include List to show multiple similar items (like open todos), and Grid similar to a List but with more legroom to show an image for each item (like a collection of icons).

For more detailed technical specifications about the List.Item.Detail component structure, properties, and implementation patterns, you may want to visit the official Raycast API reference documentation at https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface/list.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "Raycast keyboard navigation shortcuts command palette implementation"

Links: [{"title":"Keyboard Shortcuts","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/keyboard-shortcuts"},{"title":"Command Aliases and Hotkeys","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/command-aliases-and-hotkeys"},{"title":"Raycast Store: Keyboard Shortcut Sequences","url":"https://www.raycast.com/HelloImSteven/keyboard-shortcut-sequences"},{"title":"Keyboard | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/keyboard"},{"title":"Set custom hotkeys for Raycast commands | Reclaim.ai Help Center","url":"https://help.reclaim.ai/en/articles/8148933-set-custom-hotkeys-for-raycast-commands"},{"title":"Shortcuts","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/windows/shortcuts"},{"title":"Three Tips to Combine BetterTouchTool and Raycast for Simpler Keyboard Shortcuts - MacStories","url":"https://www.macstories.net/tutorials/three-tips-to-combine-bettertouchtool-and-raycast-for-simpler-keyboard-shortcuts/"},{"title":"Full Keyboard Access on Mac | Raycast","url":"https://www.raycast.com/core-features/system"},{"title":"Hotkey","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/hotkey"},{"title":"Raycast on Windows: A Keyboard‑First Command Palette for Fast Actions | Windows Forum","url":"https://windowsforum.com/threads/raycast-on-windows-a-keyboard-first-command-palette-for-fast-actions.395552/"}]

Based on the search results, here's information about Raycast's keyboard navigation shortcuts and command palette implementation:

## Command Palette Interface

Raycast's primary interface is a command palette invoked by a hotkey (default Alt+Space, customizable). Raycast is a keyboard-centric command palette that reframes the launcher as an action surface rather than a passive finder, bringing together a fast launcher, integrated clipboard history, extensible actions and automations, and built-in AI capabilities.

## Keyboard Navigation and Actions

Most Raycast windows will have an action panel with actions associated to a list item or a detail view. For developers implementing keyboard shortcuts in Raycast, the API allows you to define actions with shortcuts specifying modifier keys (such as "opt") and key values (such as "arrowUp").

## Hotkey Configuration

To make it easier to use favorite Commands, you can set up a Command Alias or Hotkey. You can only set keyboard shortcuts to contain one or multiple of the following modifier keys: Command (⌘), Option (⌥), Control (^), and Shift (⇧).

## Full Keyboard Access

With the System extension, you can change the volume, lock your screen, show/hide apps, and execute numerous commands seamlessly, enabling full keyboard access on your Mac.

The search results provide both end-user documentation and developer API references for implementing keyboard navigation in Raycast.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
